sound_type: rippling curlew call -- rapid, evenly-spaced series of 7 whistled notes: 'pi-pi-pi-pi-pi-pi-pi', ~1 second total, clear, carrying, descending slightly. One of the most diagnostic shorebird calls. E=4
ecosystem: Tidal flats, coastal mudflats, estuaries during migration. Breeds on Arctic/subarctic tundra. One of the most widely distributed shorebirds on Earth.
season: Migrant through PNW. Spring (April-May) and fall (July-September). Not a common breeder or winterer in PNW.

coordinates.description: Whimbrel on Puget Sound tidal flats during spring and fall migration. Regular migrant at Padilla Bay, Skagit Bay.

taxonomy_note.description: THIRD SCOLOPACIDAE entry. First curlew. Down-curved bill. Bill morphology progression: straight (snipe 168) > slightly decurved (Dunlin 169) > down-curved (Whimbrel 170). Begins curlew comparison pair with Long-billed Curlew (171).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Numenius from Greek 'neoumenios' (new moon) -- referencing the curved bill shape. phaeopus from Greek 'phaios' (dusky) + 'pous' (foot). Whimbrel from historical English name, possibly imitative of the call.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Numenius -- curlews. ~8 species. The largest shorebirds. Long down-curved bills. Two in PNW: Whimbrel and Long-billed Curlew.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Third Scolopacidae. First curlew. Down-curved bill enters the engine.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: ~2000 Hz per note. 7 notes per call.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: ~1 second per rippling call (7 notes). Given repeatedly in flight.
song_metrics.note_structure: Rippling call: 7 evenly-spaced whistled notes, 'pi-pi-pi-pi-pi-pi-pi'. Clear, slightly descending. Even spacing is diagnostic.
song_metrics.note_types: Rippling call (primary -- flight/alarm), alarm (drawn-out version), display song (breeding grounds -- bubbling trill).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: 7-note rippling phrase. Complete in ~1 second. Repeated at intervals during flight.
song_metrics.timbre: Clear, whistled, carrying. Wild, lonesome quality evoking vast distances. Clean tonal quality.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Strong, carrying, diagnostic. Several hundred meters range.
song_metrics.tempo: 7 notes in ~1 second = ~7 Hz note rate. Evenly spaced.
song_metrics.context: Flight call during migration. Alarm. Territory. Given frequently in flight -- the diagnostic migrant shorebird call.
song_metrics.key_property: RIPPLING CURLEW CALL -- 7 notes, ~1 second, clear whistled, diagnostic. First curlew. Down-curved bill. E=4. v1.49.310.

plumage.overall: Brown streaked overall. Distinctive head pattern: dark crown with pale central stripe, bold dark eye-stripe. Long down-curved bill. Medium-large shorebird.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Down-curved bill. Bold head stripes (crown + eye-stripe). Brown streaked body. In flight: plain wings, no wingbar.
plumage.flight_pattern: Strong, direct flight. Often gives rippling call in flight. Usually seen singly or in small groups during migration.

physical_description.overall: Length: 40-46 cm. Weight: 270-490g. Bill: 7-9 cm, strongly down-curved. Medium-large, heavy-bodied shorebird.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Down-curved bill. Head stripes. Size. Rippling flight call.
physical_description.field_id: Medium-large shorebird on tidal flats during migration. Down-curved bill. Bold head stripes. Listen for diagnostic 7-note rippling call.

nodes.title: Rippling Curlew Call -- 7-Note Diagnostic
nodes.description: Rapid, evenly-spaced series of 7 whistled notes in ~1 second. Clear, slightly descending. Instantly recognizable. One of the most diagnostic shorebird calls. The sound of vast distances.

nodes.title: Curlew Bill -- Down-Curved Morphology
nodes.description: 7-9 cm, strongly down-curved. Adapted for reaching into crevices and crab burrows. First curlew bill in the engine. Bill morphology progression: straight > decurved > down-curved.

nodes.title: Incredible Migration -- Hemispheric Voyager
nodes.description: One of Earth's great migrants. 4,000-8,000+ km between breeding and wintering. Transoceanic flights (5,000 km nonstop). Global distribution. Site fidelity to stopover locations.

nodes.title: Curlew Comparison Pair (170-171)
nodes.description: Whimbrel (170): 7-9 cm bill, moderate curlew. Long-billed Curlew (171): up to 21 cm bill, extreme curlew. Comparison pair for curlew morphology and call. Short vs long.

cross_references.relationship: CONSECUTIVE SCOLOPACIDAE! Dunlin (169) to Whimbrel (170). 3rd in sequence. Flock sound to individual call. Short bill to long bill.
SCOLOPACIDAE STREAK! Snipe (168): straight bill, mechanical sound. Whimbrel (170): curved bill, vocal call. Bill morphology progression.
CURLEW COMPARISON PAIR! Whimbrel (170): 7-9 cm bill. Long-billed Curlew (171): up to 21 cm bill. Moderate vs extreme. Rippling vs rising.
CHARADRIIFORMES SEQUENCE! Four consecutive (167-170). Plover > snipe > sandpiper > curlew. Increasing bill specialization.
